import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp

def get_observation(state):
    """Local grid patch observation - fundamentally different approach."""
    H, W = state.grid.shape[:2]
    agent_pos = state.agent.position
    
    # Create a 5x5 local view centered on agent
    view_size = 5
    half_size = view_size // 2
    
    # Initialize local view with walls (out of bounds = wall)
    local_view = jnp.full((view_size, view_size), 2.0, dtype=jnp.float32)  # WALL = 2
    
    # Fill in actual grid values where they exist
    for dy in range(-half_size, half_size + 1):
        for dx in range(-half_size, half_size + 1):
            grid_y = agent_pos[0] + dy
            grid_x = agent_pos[1] + dx
            
            # Check if position is in bounds
            in_bounds = (grid_y >= 0) & (grid_y < H) & (grid_x >= 0) & (grid_x < W)
            
            # Get tile value (default to wall if out of bounds)
            tile_val = jax.lax.select(
                in_bounds,
                state.grid[jnp.clip(grid_y, 0, H-1), jnp.clip(grid_x, 0, W-1), 0].astype(jnp.float32),
                2.0  # WALL
            )
            
            local_view = local_view.at[dy + half_size, dx + half_size].set(tile_val)
    
    # Normalize tile IDs to [0, 1] range
    normalized_view = local_view / 12.0  # Max tile ID is 12
    
    # Flatten the 5x5 view (25 features)
    flattened_view = normalized_view.flatten()
    
    # Add agent direction as one-hot (4 features)
    dir_onehot = jax.nn.one_hot(state.agent.direction, 4, dtype=jnp.float32)
    
    # Total: 25 + 4 = 29 features (much more compact than previous approaches)
    observation = jnp.concatenate([flattened_view, dir_onehot])
    
    return observation

def compute_reward(state, action, next_state):
    """Pure Manhattan distance shaping - no goal bonus to avoid reward hacking."""
    # Find goal position
    goal_mask = (state.grid[:, :, 0] == 6)
    H, W = state.grid.shape[:2]
    ys = jnp.arange(H, dtype=jnp.float32)
    xs = jnp.arange(W, dtype=jnp.float32)
    yy, xx = jnp.meshgrid(ys, xs, indexing='ij')
    
    goal_count = jnp.maximum(jnp.sum(goal_mask), 1.0)
    goal_y = jnp.sum(yy * goal_mask) / goal_count
    goal_x = jnp.sum(xx * goal_mask) / goal_count
    
    # Pure Manhattan distance improvement
    prev_pos = state.agent.position.astype(jnp.float32)
    curr_pos = next_state.agent.position.astype(jnp.float32)
    
    prev_dist = jnp.abs(prev_pos[0] - goal_y) + jnp.abs(prev_pos[1] - goal_x)
    curr_dist = jnp.abs(curr_pos[0] - goal_y) + jnp.abs(curr_pos[1] - goal_x)
    
    # Just distance improvement - let the environment handle goal detection
    reward = prev_dist - curr_dist
    
    return reward.astype(jnp.float32)
